label: 248_lcr infrastructure_climate finance_lcr_sustainable infrastructure
description: Global infrastructure needs for low-carbon, climate-resilient projects exceed $85 trillion by 2030. Climate finance from institutions like the Green Climate Fund is crucial but insufficient; blending funds and public-private partnerships are explored to bridge the gap.

newest_articles.title: Climate finance is the means to achieve climate action
newest_articles.excerpt: Finance is needed to turn climate ambition into action, to transform the energy system to eliminate greenhouse gas emissions and to make our infrastructure more resilient to climate-related events. The need for finance is especially acute in the developing world. Host Samantha Gross talks with Amar Bhattacharya of…

central_articles.title: Blending climate funds to finance low-carbon, climate-resilient infrastructure
central_articles.excerpt: The world’s core infrastructure—including our transport and energy systems, buildings, industry, and land-related activities—produce more than 60 percent of all greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions globally. By 2030 the world will need to build approximately $85 trillion in low-carbon climate-resilient (LCR) infrastructure…

central_articles.excerpt: The role of climate finance in low-carbon climate resilient infrastructure Over the next 15 years (2015-2030) the world will need to build on the order of US $75-$86 trillion in infrastructure—approximately double the estimated $50 trillion stock of infrastructure. This increases to $116.55 trillion once investments…
